mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows that months with positive PAYEMS change produce positive 20-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the Rank IC between PAYEMS change and 20-day returns is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned at the zero lower bound below 0.25 percent, persistent liquidity provision suppresses short-term risk-free rates and inflates equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the S&P 500 forward 60-day return is negative in more than 50% of observations, or the mean return is not statistically greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls indicate a self-reinforcing demand expansion where income growth directly fuels consumption, amplifying earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of contemporaneous RSAFS and PAYEMS increases fails to outperform the best single-series signal in directional accuracy, or walk-forward Rank IC for the interaction term falls below 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery regimes, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals capacity utilization normalization and operating leverage recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery months, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 50% of instances, or the positive hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in housing starts reflects strengthening residential investment and construction employment, which propagates through upstream manufacturing and credit demand to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST coincide with positive 60-day forward S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of months, or the Spearman correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day returns is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent, labor market slack diminishes sufficiently to trigger wage growth expectations and consumption confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses below 7.0%, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in at least 50% of triggered instances, or the average forward return is lower than the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating industrial production and decelerating consumer price inflation captures a positive supply shock that expands real output without triggering monetary contraction, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO acceleration and CPIAUCSL deceleration produces a negative walk-forward Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms a simple INDPRO-only signal in directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an increase in the federal funds rate signals the central bank's confidence in economic momentum, which validates earnings growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months classified as monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.1.
